value.reason: The cited page is specifically about **Automations** (scheduled/recurring background tasks) in OpenAI Codex, and worktrees are mentioned only as an *option within automations* to isolate automation changes from local work. The page does not document a general-purpose "parallel isolated workspaces" capability for working on multiple branches/copies simultaneously without collisions — it documents worktrees purely in the context of scheduled automation runs. The capability being audited (parallel isolated workspaces for simultaneous multi-branch work) is adjacent to but distinct from what this page covers (Workflows/saved automations). Furthermore, the description claims this is handled automatically for "any Git repo automation" requiring "no manual workspace setup," but the page explicitly states that worktree use is a *user choice* ("you can choose whether an automation runs in your local project or on a new worktree"), not automatic. The label "Git Worktree Isolation" partially reflects page content but only in the narrower automation context, not as a standalone parallel-workspace capability. Because the page is fundamentally about the Automations feature (an adjacent capability this cell must be distinct from), documents_capability is false.
